title: if and else
instruction:
No parentheses needed around the condition. Braces are always required.

Run this, then change x to 3 and run again.
successMessage: Rust's if looks clean without parentheses. The else is optional — omitting it means 'do nothing if false'.

title: else if chains
instruction:
Chain multiple conditions:

Run this then change `score` to 75 and 45.
successMessage: The first true condition wins. Remaining branches are skipped.

title: if as an expression
instruction:
Rust's if returns a value. Both branches must return the same type.

Run this.
successMessage: if as an expression replaces the ternary operator. The compiler enforces that both branches return the same type.

title: loop
instruction:
`loop` runs forever until `break`. It can also return a value via `break value`.

Run this.
successMessage: break count * 2 exits the loop and returns the value. That value is assigned to result. Loops as expressions are a clean Rust pattern.

title: while loop
instruction:
`while` repeats while a condition is true.

Run this.
successMessage: println!() with no arguments prints just a newline. print! outputs without a newline.

title: for with a range
instruction:
`1..=5` is an inclusive range (1 through 5). `1..5` excludes the end.

Run this.
successMessage: for with a range is idiomatic Rust for counted loops. No index variable to manage, no off-by-one risk.

title: for over a collection
instruction:
`for x in &collection` iterates over references to elements.

Run this.
successMessage: &langs borrows the array. Without &, the array would be moved into the loop and unusable afterwards.

title: continue
instruction:
`continue` skips to the next iteration.

Run this.
successMessage: continue skips even numbers. The rest of the loop body runs only for odd numbers.
